subject: Product Management
title: Microsoft Zune
description: Learn Microsoft Zune in one minute.

hook.text: Microsoft launched the Zune music player in 2006 to compete with the iPod. It arrived four years too late to matter.

buildup.text: The Zune was arguably better than the iPod in some ways: a bigger screen, FM radio, and wireless song sharing.

discovery.text: But Apple had locked in the music ecosystem: iPod + iTunes + iTunes Store. Users had already invested in Apple's world.

twist.text: Zune's wireless sharing had bizarre restrictions — shared songs expired after three plays. It felt punitive, not generous.

climax.text: Zune never exceeded 9% market share. By 2011, Microsoft discontinued it. The iPod ecosystem was unbreakable.

punchline.text: A better product can't beat an established ecosystem.

quiz.question: Why couldn't the Zune compete with the iPod?
options: Apple's ecosystem lock-in (iPod + iTunes + Store) was too strong to challenge
Zune had worse technology than the iPod
